slides.title: 3 French habits that actually work
slides.subtitle: Simple daily system for B1 → B2

slides.body: Start with 10 minutes of real French audio. Your brain needs rhythm before rules.
Pick one useful sentence and say it 10 times out loud. Fluency comes from repetition, not just notes.
Don’t collect new words forever. Reuse what you forgot yesterday.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Want a B2 routine? Comment B2 and follow @milywan_education.

caption: 3 French habits that actually work. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: Translation slows you down. Build small ready-to-use sentence blocks instead.
Use: « À mon avis… », « Je pense que… », « Ce qui est intéressant, c’est que… »
Your first goal is not perfection. It’s keeping the conversation alive.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment SPEAK if you want more French speaking prompts.

caption: Stop freezing when speaking French. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.title: TCF speaking: what B2 answers need
slides.subtitle: Structure beats memorization

slides.body: Give a clear opinion: « Je suis plutôt favorable à… »
Add one reason with: « parce que », « car », « étant donné que ».
Add one example to make your answer sound developed and natural.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment TCF and follow for more B2 speaking strategies.

caption: TCF speaking: what B2 answers need. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: Instead of « très important », use « essentiel », « primordial », or « crucial ».
Instead of « beaucoup de », use « de nombreux » or « un nombre considérable de ».
Instead of « je pense », use « j’estime que » or « je suis d’avis que ».

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment VOCAB and save this post.

caption: Replace basic French words. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: French uses liaison and linking, so words don’t always sound separate.
Native speakers reduce sounds: « je ne sais pas » can sound like « j’sais pas ».
Replay 20 seconds until you hear the details. Don’t force long podcasts too early.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment LISTEN for more French listening tips.

caption: Why French sounds too fast. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.title: Make your French writing more B2
slides.subtitle: One simple upgrade

slides.body: Basic: « C’est important parce que ça aide les gens. »
Better: « C’est essentiel, car cela permet aux gens de mieux s’adapter. »
More precise vocabulary + formal connectors = stronger writing.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment WRITE for more sentence upgrades.

caption: Make your French writing more B2. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.title: 5 connectors for B2 French
slides.subtitle: Use these in speaking and writing

slides.body: To add: « de plus », « en outre », « également ».
To contrast: « cependant », « pourtant », « en revanche ».
To conclude: « en somme », « ainsi », « pour conclure ».

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment B2 for more useful French connectors.

caption: 5 connectors for B2 French. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: You can have an accent and still be clear. Clarity matters more.
French flows in rhythm groups. Don’t stress every word equally.
Shadow one short sentence before moving to a full paragraph.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment PRONONCIATION and save this post.

caption: Pronunciation tip French learners ignore. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.title: Are you studying French or just consuming it?
slides.subtitle: This changes everything

slides.body: Passive: watching videos, saving posts, reading lists… but never producing French.
Active: writing 5 sentences, speaking for 2 minutes, correcting one mistake.
For every 20 minutes of input, produce at least 5 minutes of French.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment ACTIVE if you’re switching to active practice.

caption: Are you studying French or just consuming it?. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: French can strengthen immigration and career opportunities in Canada.
Random study is not enough. You need listening, speaking, writing, and review.
A realistic daily routine beats a huge plan you quit after 3 days.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment PR and follow for daily French support.

caption: Learning French for Canada PR?. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: Use it after emotion, necessity, doubt, and certain expressions.
Example: « Il faut que tu pratiques. »
Example: « Je suis content que tu sois là. »

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment GRAMMAR for more simple French explanations.

caption: Stop fearing the subjunctive. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.title: How to read French without translating
slides.subtitle: Read smarter, not slower

slides.body: Read for meaning first, not every unknown word.
Underline repeated words and useful expressions.
Summarize the paragraph in one French sentence.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment READ for more B2 reading drills.

caption: How to read French without translating. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: You recognize words but don’t reuse them enough.
Make your own sentence with every important word.
Review after 24 hours, 3 days, and 7 days.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment MEMORY if vocabulary disappears too fast.

caption: Why you forget French words. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: Ask follow-up questions instead of ending the conversation.
Use: « Et toi ? », « Pourquoi ? », « Comment ça ? »
React naturally: « Ah bon ? », « C’est intéressant ! »

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment CHAT for more conversation prompts.

caption: Keep a French conversation going. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.title: One mistake blocking your French
slides.subtitle: Too many resources, not enough routine

slides.body: Studying too many resources at once creates confusion.
Pick one main course or daily routine.
Use extra resources only to support your main routine.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment ROUTINE if you need a French plan.

caption: One mistake blocking your French. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: Replace « ça » with « cela » in formal writing.
Replace « plein de » with « de nombreux ».
Replace « parce que » sometimes with « car ».

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment FORMAL for more upgrades.

caption: Sound more professional in French. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: Start with a clear opinion.
Support it with one reason and one example.
Add nuance with « même si » or « cependant ».

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment ORAL for more exam structures.

caption: B2 speaking formula. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.title: Podcast method for French learners
slides.subtitle: Don’t listen once and move on

slides.body: First listen for general meaning.
Second listen for useful phrases.
Third listen and repeat 3 short sentences aloud.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment PODCAST for more listening ideas.

caption: Podcast method for French learners. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: Subject + verb + object is your base.
Add connectors carefully.
Don’t overcomplicate every sentence.

slides.title: Want more?
slides.body: Comment SENTENCE for more grammar tips.

caption: French sentence architecture. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: Is your opinion clear?
Do you have connectors?
Did you give examples?

slides.body: Comment CHECKLIST for more writing help.

caption: B2 writing checklist. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: Group words by real situations.
Use them in mini-stories.
Review words in context, not alone.

slides.body: Speaking before ready is part of learning.
Start with 30 seconds.
Repeat the same topic twice and improve it.

slides.title: The French R is not everything
slides.subtitle: Don’t let one sound block you

slides.body: One sound should not stop your speaking.
Focus on vowels and rhythm too.
Clarity matters more than sounding native.

slides.body: Comment SOUND for pronunciation tips.

caption: The French R is not everything. Save this post and use it in your French routine this week.

slides.body: You are not proving you know every word.
You are showing you can communicate clearly.
Structure your answers before adding details.

slides.body: 10 minutes listening.
10 minutes speaking or shadowing.
10 minutes writing or review.

slides.body: Basic: « Je veux apprendre le français. »
Better: « Je souhaite améliorer mon français afin d’atteindre le niveau B2. »
Precise verbs and purpose phrases make your sentence stronger.

slides.body: You improve before you notice it.
Record yourself weekly.
Compare old and new answers.

Try audio first.
Use subtitles only to verify.

Mistakes are allowed.
Communication must stay clear.
